LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL R. JOHNSON'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DENIED. THE MOTION IS PROCEDURALLY DEFECTIVE FOR IMPROPER SERVICE AND FOR FAILING TO ATTACH AN INTERLINEATED PROPOSED AMENDMENT AS REQUIRED BY CCP SECTION 471.5. THE MOTION ALSO IS WITHOUT MERIT UNDER SIMMONS VS. ALLSTATE INS. CO. (2001), 92 CAL. APP. 4TH, 1068. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Plaintiff'S Motion To Consolidate Of Cases
Off Calendar Dec-05-2008 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff Michael R. Johnson's Motion To Consolidation Of Cases (#479267 & #479270).
APPEARANCES: Michael R. Johnson, 368-5587 pltf & moving party in propria persona; Erik P. Weiss, Esq. of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney 788-1900 for deft & opposing party.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - off calendar as moot based on the Court's tentative ruling in plaintiff's motions for leave to file second amended complaint. (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Plaintiff Michael R. Johnson'S Motion For Leave To File 2nd Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-05-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF MICHAEL R. JOHNSON'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint.
APPEARANCES: Michael R. Johnson, 368-5587 pltf & moving party in propria persona; Erik P. Weiss, Esq. of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney 788-1900 for deft & opposing party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - denied. The motion is procedurally defective for improper service and for failing to attach an interlineated proposed amendment as required by CCP Section 471.5. The motion also is without merit under Simmons vs. Allstate Ins. Co. (2001), 92 Cal. App. 4th, 1068. Order signed in open court. (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
